blocks:
سُورة العاديات

مدنية عن ابن عبّاس وقتادة وقيل مكيّة عدد آيها احدى عشرة آية بالإجماع بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالْعادِياتِ ضَبْحاً ** (1) By the snorting chargers, It was said that Allah swore by the horses of the raiders (خيل الغزاة) which run and snort ضَبْحاً , and this (الضبح) is the sound of their breaths during the running.

In al-Majmaʿ, narrated from ʿAlī (peace be upon him): They are the camels when He (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) went to the Battle of Badr, stretching their necks during the journey, and they snort (تضبح), meaning they make the sound of a camel (تضبع).

And in another narration from him (peace be upon him): They are the camels from ʿArafah to Muzdalifah, and from Muzdalifah to Minā.

(٢) فَالْمُورِياتِ قَدْحاً ** (2) then those that strike fire [with their hooves], Meaning, those that strike fire (توري النار), i.e., they bring it out with their hooves from the stones of the earth. Al-Qummi He said: Their lands contained stones, so when the hooves (سنابك) of the horses trod upon them, fire would be struck (تنقدح) from them. (٣) فَالْمُغِيراتِ تغير (3) then those that raid at dawn, Their people raid (تغير) the enemy صُبْحاً at the time of dawn (الصبح). Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, they came upon them at dawn (صبّحهم) with the raid (بالغارة). (٤) فَأَثَرْنَ بِهِ نَقْعاً ** (4) raising thereby a cloud of dust, So they stirred up (فهيّجن) thereby dust (غباراً). Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, the dust (الغبرة) arose from the running (ركض) of the horses. (٥) فَوَسَطْنَ بِهِ جَمْعاً ** (5) and cleaving thereby the middle of a host, From the hosts (جموع) of the enemies. Al-Qummi said: The polytheists (المشركون) penetrated the middle (توسّط) with their host (بجمعهم). It is as if He intended by this their surrounding (احاطتهم) the polytheists (بالمشركين), or it is an error (غلط) of the scribes (الكتّاب), and the correct reading is المشركين (the polytheists). And in al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): He read فوسطن with tashdid (doubling of the sīn).

(6) إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ لِرَبِّهِ لَكَنُودٌ ** (6) Indeed man is ungrateful to his Lord, This is the answer to the oath (القسم). And al-kanūd (الكنود) is the ungrateful one (الكفور).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, He said: "Do you know who al-kanūd (الكنود) is?" They said: "Allah and His Messenger know best." He said: "Al-kanūd (الكنود) is the one who eats alone, withholds his aid (رفده), and strikes his servant (عبده)."

(٧) وَإِنَّهُ عَلى ذلِكَ لَشَهِيدٌ ** (7) and indeed He is a witness to that. It was said: He bears witness against himself to the ingratitude (الكنود) due to the manifestation of its effect upon him, or that Allah is a witness to his ingratitude (كنوده). (٨) وَإِنَّهُ لِحُبِّ الْخَيْرِ لَشَدِيدٌ ** (8) and indeed he is passionate in his love for wealth. It was said: wealth (المال), and it was said: life (الحياة). [He is] intense (لَشَدِيدٌ) due to being stingy (لبخيل) or due to being strong and excessive in it. (٩) أَفَلا يَعْلَمُ إِذا بُعْثِرَ ما فِي الْقُبُورِ ** (9) Does he not know, when what is in the graves is scattered, [When] what is in the graves (ما فِي الْقُبُورِ) is scattered (بُعْثِرَ), meaning: resurrected from the dead. (١٠) وَحُصِّلَ ما فِي الصُّدُورِ. (10) and what is in the breasts is brought out? And what is in the breasts (ما فِي الصُّدُورِ) is gathered (حُصِّلَ) and made manifest. (١١) إِنَّ رَبَّهُمْ بِهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ لَخَبِيرٌ ** (11) Indeed, on that day their Lord will be well-aware of them. [He is] Knower (لَخَبِيرٌ) of what they declared and what they concealed, and He will recompense them.

In al-Amali, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Surah. He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم dispatched Umar ibn al-Khattab in a detachment (سرّية), but he returned defeated, accusing his companions of cowardice, and they accused him of cowardice. When he reached the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He said to Ali (peace be upon him): "You are the leader of the people, so prepare yourself and whomever you wish from the horsemen of the Muhajirun and the Ansar." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم then dispatched him and said to him: "Rest during the day and travel during the night, and may the eye [of Allah] never leave you." He (the Imam) said: Ali (peace be upon him) proceeded according to what the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded him, and he marched towards them. When it was near dawn, he raided them. Then Allah revealed to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: وَالْعادِياتِ (By the snorting chargers) until the end of the Surah.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (the Imam) (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the people of Wadi al-Yabis (وادي اليابس). Twelve thousand horsemen gathered there, and they made a pact, an agreement, and a solemn covenant that no man would lag behind another, no one would abandon another, and no man would flee from his companion until they all died upon a single oath, and that they would kill Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). Then Jibra'il descended and informed Him of their story, what they had agreed upon, and what they had sworn. He commanded Him to send Abu Bakr to them with four thousand horsemen from the Muhajirun and the Ansar. So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ascended the pulpit, praised Allah, and glorified Him. Then He said: "O assembly of the Muhajirun and the Ansar! Jibra'il has informed me that the people of Wadi al-Yabis are twelve thousand strong. They have prepared themselves, agreed, and made a pact that none of them will betray his companion, nor flee from him, nor abandon him, until they kill me and my brother Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). And He commanded me to send Abu Bakr to them with four thousand horsemen. So take up your affairs, prepare for your enemy, and march towards them in the Name of Allah and His blessing on Monday, if Allah wills." The Muslims took their equipment and prepared themselves. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded Abu Bakr with His order. Among what He commanded him was that when he saw them, he should offer them Islam. If they followed, [good]; otherwise, he should confront them, kill their fighters, take their offspring captive, seize their wealth, and destroy their estates and homes. Abu Bakr and those with him from the Muhajirun and the Ansar proceeded in the best equipment and the best formation, traveling gently (سيراً رفيقاً) until they reached the people of Wadi al-Yabis. When the news of their arrival reached the people, and Abu Bakr and his companions encamped near them, two hundred men (مأتا رجل) from the people of Wadi al-Yabis came out to them, fully armed. When they met them, they said: "Who are you? Where have you come from, and where are you going? Let your leader come out to us so we may speak with him." Abu Bakr came out to them with a group of his Muslim companions and said to them: "I am Abu Bakr, the companion of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." They said: "What brought you to us?" He said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded me to offer Islam to you, and that you enter into what the Muslims have entered into. You will have what they have, and you will be subject to what they are subject to, regarding good and evil. Otherwise, war is between us and you." They said to him: "By al-Lat and al-Uzza! Were it not for a close kinship (رحم ماسّة) and a near relation, we would have killed you and all your companions with a killing that would be a tale for those who come after you. Return, you and those with you, and hope for safety, for we only seek your leader specifically, and his brother Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him)." Abu Bakr said to his companions: "O people! The enemy is many times greater than you, and better prepared than you, and your homes are far from your Muslim brothers. Let us return and inform the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم of the condition of the people." They all said to him: "You have disobeyed, O Abu Bakr, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and what He commanded you! So fear Allah and confront the people, and do not violate the saying of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." He said: "I know what you do not know, and the witness sees what the absent one does not see." So he turned back, and all the people turned back. He informed the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم of the people's statement to him and Abu Bakr's response to them. He said: "O Abu Bakr! You violated My command and did not do what I ordered you. By Allah, you were disobedient to Me in what I commanded you." Then the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم stood up and ascended the pulpit, praised Allah and glorified Him. Then He said: "O assembly of Muslims! I commanded Abu Bakr to march to the people of Wadi al-Yabis and to offer them Islam and invite them to Allah. If they accepted, [good]; otherwise, he was to confront them. He marched towards them, and two hundred men came out from them to him. When he heard their speech and how they confronted him, his chest swelled [with fear], and terror entered him because of them. He abandoned My saying and did not obey My command. Jibra'il has commanded Me, on behalf of Allah, to send Umar in his place with his companions, four thousand horsemen. So march, O Umar, in the Name of Allah, and do not act as your brother Abu Bakr acted, for he disobeyed Allah and disobeyed Me." And He commanded him with what He had commanded Abu Bakr. So Umar departed, along with the Muhajirun and the Ansar who had been with Abu Bakr, traveling moderately (يقتصد بهم) in their journey until he approached the people, being close enough to see them and be seen by them. Two hundred men came out to him and said to him and his companions the same thing they had said to Abu Bakr. So he turned back, and the people turned back with him. His heart almost flew out of fear due to what he saw of the enemy's numbers and gathering, and he returned fleeing from them. Then Jibra'il descended and informed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم of what Umar had done, and that he had turned back, and the Muslims had turned back with him. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ascended the pulpit, praised Allah and glorified Him, and informed [the people] of what Umar had done and what had occurred from him, and that he had turned back, and the Muslims had turned back with him, violating My command and disobeying My saying. When he arrived, he informed Him of the same thing his companion had informed Him of. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: "O Umar! You disobeyed Allah on His Throne, and you disobeyed Me, and you violated My saying and acted according to your own opinion (رأيك). May Allah not make your opinion ugly (لا قبّح الله رأيك). Jibra'il has commanded Me to send Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) with these Muslims, and he informed Me that Allah will grant victory (يفتح) to him and his companions." So He called Ali (peace be upon him) and advised him with what He had advised Abu Bakr and Umar, and his four thousand companions, and informed him that Allah would grant victory to him and his companions. Ali (peace be upon him) departed with the Muhajirun and the Ansar, and he traveled with them differently from the journey of Abu Bakr. That is, he was harsh (١) with them in the journey, so much so that they feared they would be exhausted from fatigue and that their mounts would become lame (تحفى دوابهم). He said to them: "Do not fear, for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded me with an order and informed me that Allah will grant victory to me and to you. So rejoice, for you are upon goodness and heading towards goodness." Their souls and hearts were comforted, and they continued on that difficult journey until they were close to them, where they could see them and be seen by them. He commanded his companions to dismount. The people of Wadi al-Yabis heard of the arrival of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) and his companions, so they sent out two hundred fully armed men (شاكين بالسلاح) to meet them. When Ali (peace be upon him) saw them, he went out to them with a group of his companions. They said to them: "Who are you? Where are you from? Where have you come from, and where are you going?" He said: "I am Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), the cousin of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, His brother, and His messenger to you. I invite you to bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger. If you believe, you will have what the Muslims have, and you will be subject to what the Muslims are subject to, regarding good and evil." They said to him: "It is you we sought, and you are our objective. We have heard your speech, so take your precaution and prepare for the fierce war (الحرب العوان). Know that we shall kill you and your companions. The appointed time between us and you is tomorrow at mid-morning (ضحوة). We have given you fair warning (اعذرنا) between us and you." Ali (peace be upon him) said to them: "Woe to you! Do you threaten me with your multitude and gathering? I seek help against you through Allah, His angels, and the Muslims. There is no power and no strength except with Allah, the Most High, the Magnificent." So they returned to their positions, and Ali returned to his position. When night fell, he commanded his companions to treat their mounts well, feed them (يقضموا), and saddle them (يُسْرِجوا). When the column of dawn broke, he prayed with the people in the darkness (بغلس) and then raided them with his companions. They did not know until the horses trampled them. Before the last of his companions arrived, he had killed their fighters, taken their offspring captive, seized their wealth, and destroyed their homes. He returned with the captives and the wealth. Jibra'il descended and informed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم of the victory Allah had granted to Ali (peace be upon him) and the community of Muslims. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ascended the pulpit, praised Allah and glorified Him, and informed the people of the victory Allah had granted to the Muslims, and informed them that only two men from them had been afflicted. He descended and went out to meet Ali (peace be upon him) with all the people of Medina from the Muslims, until He met him three miles outside Medina. When Ali (peace be upon him) saw Him approaching, he dismounted from his animal, and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم dismounted until He embraced him and kissed him between his eyes. The community of Muslims dismounted to Ali (peace be upon him) where the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had dismounted. He returned with the spoils and the captives and what Allah had provided them from the people of Wadi al-Yabis. Then Ja'far ibn Muhammad (peace be upon them both) said: "The Muslims never gained spoils like this, except perhaps from Khaybar, for it was like Khaybar." And Allah Almighty revealed this Surah on that day: وَالْعادِياتِ ضَبْحاً (By the snorting chargers). He said: By al-Adiyat (الْعادِياتِ) He means the horses running with the men, and al-Dabh (الضّبح) is their snorting in their reins and bits. فَالْمُورِياتِ قَدْحاً فَالْمُغِيراتِ صُبْحاً (Striking sparks of fire, and charging at dawn). He informed you that they raided them at dawn (صُبحاً). فَأَثَرْنَ بِهِ نَقْعاً (And raising thereby a cloud of dust). He said: He means the horses raising dust (نقعاً) in the valley. فَوَسَطْنَ بِهِ جَمْعاً إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ لِرَبِّهِ لَكَنُودٌ وَإِنَّهُ عَلى ذلِكَ لَشَهِيدٌ وَإِنَّهُ لِحُبِّ الْخَيْرِ لَشَدِيدٌ (And cleaving thereby the middle of the host. Indeed man is ungrateful to his Lord, and indeed He is a witness to that, and indeed he is passionate in his love for wealth). He said: He means the two of them (يعنيهما) who both witnessed Wadi al-Yabis (وادي اليابس) and were eager for the love of life (لحب الحياة حريصين). أَفَلا يَعْلَمُ (Does he not know) until the end of the Surah. He said: The two verses were revealed specifically concerning the two of them (فيهما خاصّة), as they concealed evil intentions (ضمير السّوء) and acted upon them. So Allah informed Him of their news and their actions.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah al-Adiyat and persists in its recitation, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will resurrect him specifically with the Commander of the Faithful (prayers and peace of Allah be upon him) on the Day of Resurrection, and he will be in his bosom (حجره) and among his companions, if Allah Almighty wills.

footnote (١) *Al-'Anf* (*العنف*), with the *ayn* having three possible vowelizations: the opposite of gentleness (*الرفق*).
